title: Telegram node fails to send message to channel
symptom: When attempting to send a message to a Telegram channel using the Telegram node, n8n returns a 'Bad Request: chat not found' error.
applies_when: Bot is an admin of the channel
not_this_if: Sending messages to individual chats works
root_cause: The error occurs because the chat ID for the Telegram channel is not correctly formatted or is incorrect. Telegram channels require a specific format for their chat IDs, typically starting with a prefix of '-100'.
fix_steps: Open a web browser and navigate to the Telegram API URL: https://api.telegram.org/bot{bot_token}/sendMessage?chat_id={chat_id}&text=hello, replacing {bot_token} and {chat_id} with your actual values.
Ensure the chat ID for your channel starts with '-100'.
If the request is successful, use the verified chat ID in the Telegram node settings in n8n.
agent_instruction: Verify the chat ID format for your Telegram channel and update the Telegram node with the correct ID.
